framework.name: OAuth 2.1 Single Trust Domain Limitation
framework.description: The whitepaper explicitly states OAuth 2.1 + OIDC works well within single trust domains with synchronous operations but 'may fall short in scenarios which are cross-domain, highly autonomous, or asynchronous.' This confirms the boundary problem but doesn't provide specific failure modes beyond 2 hops.
framework.raw_quote: Existing OAuth 2.1 and OpenID Connect frameworks, when used with AI agents, work well within single trust domains with synchronous agent operations...but may fall short in scenarios which are cross-domain, highly autonomous, or asynchronous
